entry.title: Re: FILTER Operation with NE (Not Equal to) in WHERE Clau...
entry.content:
With this code am aiming to find any record which is not in common between the above 2 Internal Tables based on a comparison using the common CHARACT field and FILTER operation. And the thing is any of the Tables here can have record counts on a greater side in comparison to the other Table. So, in such situations FILTER won't work is my question? Since you can see that in my example case that entry is there common with both the Tables and it should not have been deduced as a result of that FILTER query.

Thanks,

Sijin

entry.title: Is it possible to have multiple SAML identities on the same account/UID?
entry.content:
Can we have more than one SAML provider for the same user in CDC? We are considering to implement two different SAML federations and we want to know if users will be able to use both of them anytime by using the account linking feature. Additionally is the account linking with a social (Google, etc...) supported for a users who have a SAML/OIDC identity?

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to have multiple SAML identities on th...
entry.content:
Having multiple federated  identities for a single account is not supported as this type of account linking poses a security risk. A single UID can have one OIDC identity and one SAML identity linked, meaning it's possible to have one of each type associated with the same account.

  • Linking social accounts to federated identities is disabled by default, however you can use  accounts.setPolicies API to manually enable it.
  • Refer to the following SAP Note for more details:   3323416 - Unable to link account with an account that was registered via OIDC OP

entry.title: Re: FILTER Operation with NE (Not Equal to) in WHERE Clau...
entry.content: @Sijin_Chandran what you need based on your description then is to perform the union of the two sets and then subtract the intersection because only executing a single FILTER will miss rows that do not exist in the filtered set, but do exist in the set against which the filter is executed.

entry.title: Re: Special characters for projects - checkboxes not appe...

entry.title: Error solrserver 9.7 - not included on Sap Commerce Cloud 2211.41
entry.content:
Solrsever version 9.7 is not included on SAP Commerce Cloud 2211.41

However, version 9.7 is still recommended and is valid until February 2026, how can version 9.7 be used in 2211.41?

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Process accelerators
entry.content: Hi Virendar, the recommended approach is to use Signavio Process Navigator. After selecting the solution scenario, you can compare different versions to see the delta—highlighting new, updated, and deprecated solution processes. We are currently working on enabling selective import through the Value Accelerator Library, which will allow you to install only the relevant processes. While lifecycle management of reference content within Signavio is not yet supported, it is an area we are actively exploring.

entry.title: Need BW content related to CDS - I_GoodsMovementDocumentDEX
entry.content:
Need to understand if there are any standard BW content for the CDS view I_GoodsMovementDocumentDEX?

#BWCONTENT #GOODSMOVEMENT #MM

entry.title: Re: Create API Provider in SAP Integration Suite for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hello @adrito_brinca ,

I'm facing the same issue. I'm getting the same 403 Forbidden error on every actual request, even when using the test console in the Integration Suite.

However, I’m confident that the network connection itself is working. The "Test Connection" in the API Provider is successful and also shows up in the Cloud Connector logs (which is not true for any actual request). Additionally, the defined Catalog Service seems to be functioning correctly, as I'm able to retrieve and list all OData services from the on-premise system.

Did you manage to fix this problem?

Best regards,
Michael Barborka

entry.title: Re: Statement of Direction - SAP Data Services as of July 2024
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team, Thanks for detailed blog on DS and its statement of direction.

W.r.t to DS 4.3 (SAP BODS 4.3) I understand that the Mainstream maintenance support has been extended and now available till Dec'2026. Post that customer has two options available.

Option 1: Invest & Upgrade to DS 2025 version but unfortunately its Mainstream maintenance support ends by Dec'2027 which means we do upgrade, stability the environment and again are forced to do another cycle of upgrade if we want to stay in Mainstream maintenance support.  

Q-1: Why is DS 2025 having such a short mainstream maintenance duration and forcing customers to re-do and re-invest in upgrade? 

 

Option 2: Do nothing and auto shift to CSM support for CY 2027.

Q-2: Will this CSM support take care of product and other ETL issues or is it limited to Security fixes with CVSS >7 only? If it does support the product and related ETL issues, then are there some limitations that the customer needs to be aware of?

Regards,

Premkishan Chourasia

Regards,

Johannes

entry.title: Integration Process to send the Multiple incoming payloads as a single file
entry.content:
Hi All,

Currently I'm facing issue with an integration process which is developed in PI/PO.

In that PI/PO integration process the incoming payloads are collected and grouped based on the "LogicalSystem" element present in the incoming payload. This flow triggers automatically when the message count reaches to 250 or wait time = 5 mins. Then grouped messages are passed to a message mapping to convert them into single XML file sends to the target system. 

How can implement this integration process in SAP CPI. Kindly help me with Process 

Thanks in advance

Regards

Rahul

entry.title: Location not getting created when Vendor (Carrier) drop to EWM in HANA environment
entry.content:
Issue Description:

When a new carrier/vendor is sent to the S/4HANA EWM system via IDocs (BD14) from SAP ECC, the system successfully creates the Business Partner (BP). However, it does not automatically generate the corresponding location (/SAPAPO/LOC3). This behavior differs from the non-HANA EWM system, where the location is created automatically.

Current Workaround:

Currently, we are using the report /SAPAPO/CREATE_LOCATION as a manual workaround to generate the missing location.

Actions Taken:

We reviewed SAP Knowledge Base articles and found SAP Note 2909245 and 3249598.

As suggested, we implemented the relevant BAdI with an empty implementation, also added the config.

Despite this, the location is still not being created automatically when a new carrier is sent to EWM via IDoc.

Request:

We are looking for a solution or any existing SAP Note or configuration that can be implemented to ensure that locations are created automatically in S/4HANA EWM (as in the non-HANA setup).

  • Could you please advise on the following:
  • Any additional BAdIs or configuration steps required?
  • Whether any further relevant SAP Notes exist?
  • If this is a known limitation in S/4HANA EWM and whether SAP recommends a different integration or enhancement approach?

Thanks,

Rajeev Sinha

entry.title: APPGYVER Embedded Map View not rendering
entry.content:
• Problem: The built-in Map component no longer renders on any platform.

  • Tested On:

    • Existing app

    • New blank app (default map settings)

    • AppGyver Web Preview

    • AppGyver Android Preview

    • Generated APK (Android)

  • Symptoms:

    • Blank space where map should render

    • No console errors or loading indicators

  • Worked Before: Yes, up until aproximally 15th of June.

  • Urgency: Critical, app feature depends on built-in map.

Please advise if this is a known issue or when a fix is expected.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Example of a HANA Hierarchy Query for Incentive Manag...
entry.content:
I was finally able to figure out the solution.  Took me awhile and Phil's answer definitely helped.

SELECT DISTINCT
h.hierarchy_rank AS rank,
h.hierarchy_level AS level,
h.node_id AS ruleelementownerseq,
h.parent_id AS managerseq,
pos.payeeseq,
part.firstname || ' ' || part.lastname AS fullname
FROM HIERARCHY (
SOURCE (
SELECT
pos.ruleelementownerseq AS node_id,
pos.managerseq AS parent_id,
1 AS ord
FROM tcmp.cs_position pos
WHERE pos.removedate = TO_DATE('01/01/2200', 'MM/DD/YYYY')
)
START WHERE ruleelementownerseq = (
SELECT ruleelementownerseq
FROM tcmp.cs_position
WHERE payeeseq = 4503599627371743
AND removedate = TO_DATE('01/01/2200', 'MM/DD/YYYY')
)
SIBLING ORDER BY ord
ORPHAN IGNORE
) AS h
JOIN tcmp.cs_position pos
ON pos.ruleelementownerseq = h.node_id
AND pos.removedate = TO_DATE('01/01/2200', 'MM/DD/YYYY')
JOIN tcmp.cs_participant part
ON part.payeeseq = pos.payeeseq
AND part.removedate = TO_DATE('01/01/2200', 'MM/DD/YYYY')
ORDER BY h.hierarchy_rank

entry.title: Re: Business Processes Monitoring with SAP Cloud ALM

entry.title: Re: Datasphere & SAC Users and Activities Source Tables

entry.title: Default date filters fail to parse in mockserver
entry.content:
I have a date filter in my Fiori Elements page that is set up to have a default value like this:

"creationDate": {"settings":{"defaultValues":[{"operator": "TODAYFROMTO","values": [30,0]}]}}

The page sends a request to the backend like this:

GET LineItems?$count=true&$filter=creationDate%20ge%202025-05-27T00:00:00.0000000-07:00%20and%20creationDate%20le%202025-06-26T23:59:59.9990000-07:00\

This works fine on our java services, but fails to process on mock server on my MacBook. It works fine when the test is run as part of our build pipeline. The error message is:

NotAllInputParsedException: Redundant input, expecting EOF but found: T00
at RecognizerEngine.ruleFinallyStateUpdate

To emphasize, it works fine everywhere except my development environment.

Has anyone experienced the same problem? Any suggestions to resolve it?

entry.title: Re: Alternative CoA - G/L account type
entry.content: As far as I tested in Public Cloud system - it doesn't matter. All the settings, required fields etc. are taken from the operational CoA GL account. I created entire alternative CoA by copying every account from 2 samples - one for balance, one for P&L, without adjusting anything other than GL name. Postings work fine, even when Fiori setting is done to use alternative GL as input account number while posting.

entry.title: In intercompany Transactions i need the changes as below
entry.content:
Scenario 1: Company 1 owes to company 2 $500 for the insurance premium
What's happening:
Company 1 -
Dr. Interco Receivable $500 Cr. Cash $500
Fairway -
Dr. Cash $500 Cr. Interco Payable $500
What I'd expect:
Wayfair -
Dr. Interco Payable $500 Cr. Cash $500
Fairway -
Dr. Cash $500 Cr. Interco Receivable $500

Scenario 2: Company 2 owes Company 1 $100 for invoices paid on its behalf
What's happening:
Wayfair -
Dr. Cash $100 Cr. Interco Payable $100
Fairway -
Dr. Interco Receivable $100 Cr. Cash $100
What I'd expect:
Wayfair -
Dr. Cash $100 Cr. Interco Receivable $100
Fairway -
Dr. Interco Payable $100 Cr. Cash $100

sap.ui.define(

        return Component.extend("testforimg.Component", {
            metadata: {
                manifest: "json"
            }
           
        });
    }
);
 
thanks again

entry.title: One of SAP TM's hidden gems: The Load Utilization Determination Component
entry.content:
I've found that in SAP Transportation Management, some very useful components don't always get the attention they deserve. This clearly applies to the Load Utilization Determination component, and this short blog post intends to rectify this somewhat.

What It Does

Simply put, this component answers an important question: "What transportation resources will I need for this set of products?" It does this by combining Package Building and Load Consolidation capabilities to run a simulation for a set of products and resources. The component doesn't actually create shipments or change your data - it just shows you what would be needed to move your products. The following result screenshot of test report /SCMTMS/TEST_PLN_LUD illustrates this, by listing the resources with utilization percentages for the packages (pallets) which were placed by the load consolidation components. The pallets themselves where created for the products using the unified package building component:

Practical Applications and Business Value

This tool helps in several business situations:

  • Resource Forecasting: Planning your transportation needs for upcoming sales periods based on expected orders, helping you secure the right carriers and vehicles ahead of time.

  • Bid Preparation/Sales order processing: When responding to customer requests, you can quickly estimate what transportation you'll need, allowing for more accurate cost estimates.

  • Scenario Planning: Test different approaches by seeing how changes in packaging or routing might affect your resource needs.

An example might look like this: A electronics distributor receives a rush order: 200 laptops, 150 monitors, and 300 accessories going to Walldorf. Before committing to same-day delivery, they run LUD:

  • Input: Product quantities + available truck types
  • Output: "You'll need 2 standard trucks at 85% capacity, or 1 large truck at 60% capacity"
  • Decision: Quote customer for large truck option with room for additional orders.

Getting Started

Setting up this component requires some basic configuration work with your planning profile (including load consolidation and unified package building settings) and master data. Note 3618303 provides basic information on how to consume this component in your custom project. The component is available for TM 9.6 and S/4HANA 1809 onwards.

Other options

Consulting solution

If a custom implementation is not an option for you, you could also take a look at the consulting solution for Transport Load Consolidation, which has implemented a process that consumes this component and integrates into sales order and delivery processes. Please refer to note 3045005 for further information on this solution.

Standard solution

As I mentioned above, we currently have no standard solution that embeds this functionality in a TM process. We do have plans in that direction, so if you have requirements or feedback around this we would love to hear from you.

entry.title: SAP Query - Issue with missing fields on Selection Screen (MEBANF)
entry.content:
We are facing an issue with missing fields on Selection Screen of SAP query MEBANF after regenerate the program from SQ01 (Query->More Functions->Generate Program) where as result some of the fields from selection screen are not been displayed anymore (e.g.: Plant).

We already did the following actions:

1. SQ02: Infoset /SAPQUERY/MEBANF  ->  Generate InfoSet and also Adjust (InfoSet->More Functions->Adjust)
2. SE36: Verify selection fields on LDB used by above InfoSet
3. SQ01: Query MEBANF  ->  Verify selection fields and performed Adjust and Generate Program

All above objects were compared (all properties, tabs, definitions) with other system under same version and been SAP starndard query it looks all the same.

Any other way to restore missing fields from selection screen generate by Query ?

entry.title: Tiles and dashboard are not accessible
entry.content:

do you know if this is still valid that you new customer instances do not have access to tiles and dashboards? s. https://me.sap.com/notes/0003427766

I'm encountering the problem that I cannot add dashboards and tiles to a customer instance even though I have granted all necessary permissions.

I have access to dashboards, but cannot add it to the instance. In addition to that I cannot add a new one from report center. 

 

Could you please advise? 

 

Cheers,

Gunnar

entry.title: Formatted Search Help
entry.content:
Hi, 
I have a very simple formatted search that isnt returning a value.

I have a UDT called UDT WEBSITE_INV with 3 fields.

U_ItemCode         U_Bag_Inv     U_Crate_Inv
ABC1                       100                200

I added 2 fields to OITM - AIS_BAG_WEB_INV and AIS_CRATE_WEB_INV

I have a simple query but this isnt returning any results, even when I click on the magnifying glass nothing happens

SELECT T0."U_Bag_Inv" FROM "@AIS_WEBSITE_INV" T0
WHERE T0."U_ItemCode" = $[$5.0.0]

SELECT T0."U_Crate_Inv" FROM "@AIS_WEBSITE_INV" T0
WHERE T0."U_ItemCode" = $[$5.0.0]

Can anyone tell me what I am doing wrong?

After the Upload file process and Data Transfer process I am getting the error attached .

Please find attached the file and the snapshot of the error .

Can someone please guide me and let me know what is going wrong.

Regards.

SM

entry.title: Problema para exibir códigos de imposto (MWSKZ) na MIRO?
entry.content:
Recentemente, enfrentei um desafio curioso em um cliente. Após a criação de um novo código de imposto (IVA), percebi que ele simplesmente não aparecia na lista suspensa da transação MIRO. O mais intrigante? Não era só o novo código — outros IVAs já existentes também estavam invisíveis.

Começou então a clássica jornada investigativa: consultei documentações no SAP for Me, revisei todas as configurações standard recomendadas pela SAP (inclusive a Nota 2122460) e... nada. Tudo parecia estar em ordem. A dúvida persistia: por que apenas alguns códigos apareciam? Confesso que, até então, nunca tinha passado por isso em projetos anteriores — mas sempre há uma primeira vez.

Com apoio de um Expert ABAP, iniciamos o debug e — voilà! — encontramos a raiz do problema: um enhancement customizado no módulo de função HELP_VALUES_GET_WITH_TABLE.

Essa função é bastante utilizada nas versões anteriores ao S/4HANA para personalizar a ajuda de pesquisa — o famoso F4 Help.

A solução envolveu revisar esse enhancement e considerar a configuração sugerida na Nota 2122460, ou a substituição da função para F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST, já que a HELP_VALUES_GET_WITH_TABLE está obsoleta no S/4HANA.

Se você estiver enfrentando algo parecido, recomendo consultar as seguintes notas SAP:

  • 2122460 – MIRO/MIR4/MIR7: Códigos de imposto ausentes na lista suspensa
  • 3213274 – Substituição de HELP_VALUES_GET_WITH_TABLE

Abraços
Geziel R Cruz

entry.title: HCM Brasil: pré-requisitos de Support Package para implementação de SAP Notes – 2026/2027
entry.content:
Olá pessoal,

Como todos os anos, é hora de planejar a atualização dos Support Packages do seu sistema HCM Brasil.

A atualização anual dos Support Packages para HCM Brasil é obrigatória e fundamental para manter seu sistema funcionando sem problemas e em conformidade com as leis e regulamentações.

Manter seu sistema atualizado traz uma série de vantagens, incluindo:

  • Acesso às novas funcionalidades e melhorias: As atualizações de Support Packages trazem novas funcionalidades, correções de erros e otimizações de desempenho, garantindo a melhor experiência possível. Também traz correções de vulnerabilidades de segurança, protegendo seu sistema contra ameaças e garantindo a confidencialidade dos seus dados.
  • Compatibilidade com as SAP Notes: A atualização garante que você possa implementar as SAP Notes, que contêm correções importantes, atualizações legais e melhorias de segurança. A partir de Maio de 2026, a aplicação de novas notas para os componentes SAP_HRCBR e S4HCMCBR exigirá Support Packages da versão 2024 ou superior.
  • Prevenção de problemas: Manter seu sistema atualizado ajuda a evitar potenciais problemas de compatibilidade e garante a estabilidade das suas operações.

IMPORTANTE 
 
A partir de Maio de 2026, sistemas com Support Packages anteriores a Maio/2024 não poderão implementar SAP Notes referentes aos componentes SAP_HRCBR e S4HCMCBR. Isso significa que você não receberá correções, novas funcionalidades e, principalmente, atualizações legais, colocando suas operações em risco.
 
A tabela abaixo detalha os níveis mínimos de Support Package por componente para 2026-2027:

Para mais detalhes, recomendações e informações específicas sobre cada componente, acesse nosso WorkZone.

Próximos passos:

Não deixe para a última hora! Recomendamos que você inicie o planejamento da sua atualização o quanto antes.

Compartilhe este importante aviso com seus colegas e siga a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para se manter atualizado.

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blogpost no espaço abaixo. 

Atenciosamente,

Anderson Marques

entry.title: Re: Understanding ABAP Pipeline Engine used for Data Integration in Datasphere
entry.content:
Excellent.

Just the information I needed to understand how CDC works in Datasphere replication flows.

I encourage you to do the following on how to monitor this process on the source system where the CDS View resides, the basis of the extraction.

I.e. how to use the transactions of CDC Monitoring, Data Buffer Monitor,... as well as the roles needed to access the functionality of these tcodes (DHCDCMON, DHRDBMON).

entry.title: No Attachments in Screen Maintenance Requests Fiori App
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I have created maintenance notifications along with attachments via custom code. The attachments are successfully visible in the IW22 transaction, and corresponding entries exist in the TOA01 table.

However, when accessing the same notification through the Fiori app "Screen Maintenance Requests" (App ID: F4072), the attachments are not displayed.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue or is aware of a potential resolution? Any guidance on how to ensure attachments appear correctly in the Fiori app would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: cf -login Authenticating... {"error":"invalid_grant"...
entry.content:
You can try logging in using your P-user ID (e.g., P200XXXXXXX) instead of your email address. This often resolves the issue because the Cloud Foundry CLI prefers the user ID format (P/D/I) over email when authenticating.

Use the same password that you use to log in to your SAP Universal ID (or the one you used while creating the BTP trial account).

So, the login command would be:

 

bash
CopyEdit
 
cf login -a https://api.cf..... and 
Email: P2XXXXXXXX  and 
Password:<Your universal of global account id password>

 This usually works when email-based login or --sso fails.

entry.title: How to facilitate collective invoicing for service orders in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Collective invoicing allows you to combine multiple billing documents into a single invoice for a customer. For the sales processes, there is the scope item BKZ (Sales Order Processing with Invoice List and Collective Billing) that covers the standard process for collective invoicing. In this blog post, I would like to give you an overview of how something similar can be setup to facilitate collective invoicing for the service order management process.

In the standard service order management process, the system creates a separate invoice for each service order. This can however be adjusted to allow for collective invoicing of Billing document requests (BDR) from multiple service orders.

In SSCUI 102762 (Define Copying Control for Sales Document to Billing Document) and 102764 (Define Copying Control for Billing Document to Billing Document), the standard settings are as follows, which causes the field reference number to be derived from the value ‘A’, which represents the field “Customer Reference”. This is a field from the sales order, and in service orders, this defaults to the value of the Service Document ID. Due to this standard setting, billing documents created from multiple service orders will always have a different value for the Reference Number field (which corresponds to the respective service order number)

 

 

Due to this, billing multiple service orders will default to the creation of separate billing documents, due to the split criteria fields being different between the billing documents.

 

This billing split criteria can now be bypassed by changing the assignment and reference number values to the following, such that these values are derived from the actual value of the current billing document number instead.

 

Make the same change for the SSCUI 102764 for the following combination of source and target billing doc types:

For fixed price service orders, configure the same steps shown above for the following combinations:

SSCUI 102762:

 

 

SSCUI 102764:

 

 

The end result would look like this, where the system generates a single invoice for multiple service orders:

When testing web-service interface 'ManageCustomerIn' (I want to change customer master data) getting this error message: The value ? is not a valid UUID in the format "hhhhhhhh-hhhh-hhhh-hhhh-hhhhhhhhhhhh"

I need to replace in query value ? with UUID. But where can I get it from?

entry.title: Condition Contract Sample Request Payload For API
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

I am working on API_CONDITION_CONTRACT - SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Thanks,

Arindam Sur

entry.title: Re: 1h2025 - Include Inactive Employees in the search- ac...
entry.content:
Hi Kris, 

If I want to find performance forms for leavers, the way I do it is via 'Detailed Document Search'

  • Go to the Reporting module
  • Click on Switch to Classic View
  • Classic Reporting > Detailed Document Search
  • In the "Employee Document Folder Search" section, select the Completed Folder, and click Find User... to search for the user, insuring that All Users is selected under More Options (so that it includes leavers)

A bit cluncky, but it works!

Thanks, 
Jack

entry.title: "Error while parsing an XML stream" while trying to create customer for Business partner
entry.content:
Screenshot: Error in Preview mode of SAP Build while trying to create Business partner & customers.

I tried to create Business partner and business partner role using "Create Record" by applying the following formula.

SET_KEY(SET_KEY(data.A_BusinessPartner1,"to_BusinessPartnerAddress", [ {BusinessPartner: params.BPID, AddressID: params.AddressID, HouseNumber: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.HouseNumber, StreetName: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.StreetName, CityName: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.CityName, Country: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.Country, PostalCode: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.PostalCode} ] ), "to_BusinessPartnerRole", [ {BusinessPartnerRole: "FLCU00"}, {BusinessPartnerRole: "FLCU01"} ] )

Then, I have put an ALERT which gives me the Business Partner created success message.

Then, in my logic, I have used another "Create Record" to create customer for this Business Partner using the following formula.

SET_KEY({BusinessPartner: pageVars.BusinessPartnerNumber},"to_Customer", [{Customer: "", CustomerAccountGroup: "ZBLD"}])

Over here, I am trying to use the same Business Partner number which got generated from the previous CREATE RECORD and had saved it into a page variable. This create record fails and triggers a toast giving me the above screenshot error.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch in Mannheim (1 of 2) - Let's chat Joule and all things SAP...
entry.content:
It's time to meetup in Mannheim!!!  While our SAP developer event is internal only, we are going to gather together with a relaxed Monday evening get-together and all are welcome! 🎊

📆When?
🕔 starting from 18:30 (6:30pm) on Monday, June 30

📍Where?
Augusta Restaurant Mannheim Germany 

🌤️ What to expect:
- A wonderful place for drinks or food
- chance to network and connect for those attending the SAP internal developer event and anyone else in the SAP ecosystem looking to drop in and say hi
- à la carte food & drinks 🍔🍷 (pay your own way)

🎟️ Who’s invited?
Everyone! Whether you’re an SAP employee attending the internal event or an SAP enthusiast - come hang out! 💬👋

Please hit the YES button so we know how many to expect.  See you soon!!!

entry.title: Re: Recruiting APIs
entry.content:
Please see my response below:

  • The posting link is not available in the API. This is a current limitation. Could you please share the use case?

  • You should be able to filter based on the picklist fields. If it’s a custom field, ensure it is reportable; otherwise, it won’t be filterable.

  • You should be able to view the picklist field values. Are they not available in the API dictionary?

Thanks,

Sanjeet

entry.title: SAP Developer News June 26th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

PODCAST: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/06/26/sap-developer-news-june-26th-2025/ 

TechEd Registration is Live 

  • TechEd Registration Page: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html 

 

SAP CodeTalk with Matt Harding 

  • SAP CodeTalk with Matt Harding - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZLWMBdknrIY 
  • SAP CodeTalk playlist - https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQB4SrWDL_VpeZnDJ1pcLRpA&si=1AbGH_J9j2b5dYmr 

 

What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud 2025/Q2 

  • What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – June 2025: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-june-2025/ba-p/14135545 
  • New Machine Learning and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q2: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/new-machine-learning-and-ai-features-in-sap-hana-cloud-2025-q2/ba-p/14136079 
  • Calculation View Features of 2025 QRC2: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/calculation-view-features-of-2025-qrc2/ba-p/14130552 

 

InnerSource Summit 2025 CfP open until 15 July 

  • CfP form https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSfWKkRDWIYN8eTOMlxONZp23-_i9nnAfqSJm26QCdzS5NtO9w/viewform  
  • InnerSource Summit 2025 details https://innersourcecommons.org/events/isc-2025/ 

 

CodeJam Roadshows 

  • Singapore – ABAP Cloud https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/sap-codejam-abap-sap-btp-abap-environment-amp-rap-singapore/ev-p/14112313 
  • Singapore – SAP GenAI - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core-singapore/ev-p/14112295 
  • Kuala Lumpur – SAP Build - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/sap-build-create-apps-processes-and-sites-without-code-kuala-lumpur/ev-p/14112261 
  • Bangkok – SAP GenAI - https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core-bangkok-thailand/ev-p/14112276 
  • SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2025 - Latin America edition 🇧🇷🇵🇪🇲🇽🇨🇴(July 25th - Aug 12th 2024): https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-roadshow-2025-latin-america-edition-july-25th-aug-12th-2025/ba-p/14093930 

 

 CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 TechEd Registration Is Live 

1:16 CodeTalk with Matt Harding 

2:04 What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud 2025/Q2 SAP 

4:02 InnerSource Summit 2025 CfP open until 15 July 

4:48 Update from the US AI CodeJam Roadshow 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for June the 26th, 2025.  

[Rich & Thomas] Hey, everybody. SAP TechEd registration is live. That's right. So if you want to get registered early and get a discount, make sure you get out there for the early nerd. Early nerd. Early nerd discount and register today. What you can expect at TechEd this year, great things like the Community Clubhouse. We want to make it bigger and better than ever. We want to bring back that community feel that we had at TechEds of years past. We'll have hands-on sessions. We'll have lectures, community talks, strategy talks, all kinds of information for you guys to take home and use to make yourselves better as developers and architects. Yeah, and of course, if you can't make it to Berlin for the big in-person TechEd, selective sessions will be streamed online on the virtual platform as well. And we'll have TechEd on tour. We've already announced some of the locations and dates with more coming soon as well. So hopefully, no matter where you are in the world, we're going to bring you some form of TechEd this year. And we look forward to seeing all of you there.  

[Mamikee] Hi, everyone. We have a brand new SAP Code Talk. I had the pleasure of sitting down with SAP mentor Matt Harding, where we covered so much from his early days in audio engineering to diving deep into RAP, clean core and ABAP best practices. and also why musicians make great developers. Now, he shared how his creative mindset shapes his approach to architecture and development. We spoke a little bit about the evolving of SAP community and how events like CodeTalk and TechEd are keeping developers inspired and connected. And we went into so much more. Now, you can check out this CodeTalk on our SAP Developers YouTube channel, and the link to this episode will be in the description below. You don't want to miss it.  

[Witalij] As we reach the midpoint of 2025, the second release of SAP HANA Cloud for the year is now available. Here is a quick look at the key innovations relevant for developers and data scientists among you. In SAP HANA Cloud Central, the main administrative tool, the SQL console has been enhanced to improve usability and functionality. The messages and history tabs have been merged into a single, customizable tab.There is now also an option to automatically format SQL for SAP HANA database connections, helping streamlining the developer experience. This release also brings several important enhancements in the area of embedded machine learning and AI. First, expanded support for tabular AI functions, including vector data analysis with AutoML. Second, a new text embedding module version is now available supporting more languages and optimized for short text scenarios. Newly supported languages include Japanese, Italian, or Chinese. Additionally, the default token length has been increased to 512. And third, capabilities for experiment tracking and task scheduling have also been introduced. For more details, including the Jupyter notebook that demonstrates these features, see the blog post by product manager Christoph Morgan. Following the initial release of the Knowledge Graph engine in Q1, we are now expanding its functionality. The new validate query form allows users to validate RDF data against constraints defined using the shapes, containing language, or shackle. As always, we continue to invest in graphical view modeling in SAP HANA Cloud. This release introduces new features you can explore in the blog post by product manager Jan Zauke. Examples of these features are available in the GitHub repository as well. For a visual summary of the release highlights, check out a short video featuring product manager Thomas Hammer. Links are in the description.  

[DJ] the inner source summit 2025 is on the 13th of November this year it's a special 10th anniversary affair which will be held simultaneously in three locations spanning three global time zones in New York, Berlin and Yokohama if you want to participate specifically give a presentation then note that the call for presenters is open and closes on the 15th of July. All the presentations are to be around 20 minutes and given live. What are you waiting for? Head over to the CFP form and submit your proposal for a chance to speak at this great event.  

[Rich & Thomas] Hey folks, Rich Heilman and Thomas Jung here. Yeah, we're in the SAP Palo Alto offices this week, as part of our US AI CodeJam Roadshow. We were in New York on Monday. We're here in California on Wednesday. And Friday, I'll be in Houston, Texas with our coworker, Mamikee. And we're covering the AI Code Jam. That's right. That's not the only thing we've got going on. What other do we have coming up? We have more roadshows coming up, right? We have roadshows in India, in Southeast Asia. And then we have our Latin America Roadshow, which I will be a part of, as well as Antonio and Josh and me, Mr. Jung. Well, I'm only coming to Mexico, and then you guys are hitting all the other locations. Too many to remember this thing today. But as always, you can check the description below for all the links if any of those events sound interesting to you. So we'll see everybody on the road, won't we? See you there. All right.

entry.title: Re: Candidate and Manager need to sign Offer Letter (with...
entry.content:
Unfortunately, this is not feasible without DocuSign. Instead, you can use the offer approval functionality to get approval from the manager, and then proceed to extend the offer letter.

Thanks,

Sanjeet

It's time to meetup (again) in Mannheim!!!  While our SAP developer event is internal only, we are going to gather together with a relaxed Tuesday evening get-together for drinks and all are welcome! 🎊

📆When?

📍Where?

Taproom Jungbusch


🌤️ What to expect:
- A wonderful place for drinks (no food offered at location so eat earlier 😉
- chance to network and connect for those attending our internal event and anyone else in the SAP ecosystem looking to drop in and say hi
- a wonderful local taproom🍺 (pay your own way)

🎟️ Who’s invited?
Everyone! Whether you’re an SAP employee attending the internal event or an SAP enthusiast - come hang out! 💬👋

Please hit the YES button so we know how many to expect.  See you soon!!!

entry.title: newcomer student
entry.content: I am a Master’s student in Logistics Management at the National School of Commerce and Management (ENCG) in Casablanca, Morocco. I am passionate about supply chain optimization and digital transformation, and I am actively preparing to pursue a career in SAP SCM consulting.

entry.title: Re: Screen Invisible
entry.content:
screen-active = 0 

screen-invisible = 1

It works!

entry.title: Re: Ready to hire status automatically closing job req
entry.content:
In Provisioning, under "Edit Applicant Status," the status category needs to be updated. Currently, it appears to be configured as "Onboard" or "Hired," which is causing the requisition to close automatically when a candidate is moved to the "Ready to Hire" stage. You can change the status category to "Miscellaneous" to prevent the requisition from closing at that stage.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Project System work in progress accrued income
entry.content:
Are you using Resource Related Billing? You can't use 14 or 15 without it.

Are you entering SAP Budget?  That is not the same thing as Plan Cost, they are 2 separate sets of values.  SAP Budget is a spending limit and not associated with a Cost Element.  Plan Cost is entered on a Cost Element.  

To analyze your configuration I suggest you contract with an experienced PS consultant who know RA.

entry.title: Re: What is the best practice when adding a candidate in...
entry.content:
Ideally, when a candidate is manually added to the database, the first notification they receive will include the password reset link. You can check in Recruiting Email Triggers whether the "Manual Candidate Creation Notification" is enabled. If this is triggered in advance, the candidate will already have a password by the time they receive the "Invite to Apply" email, allowing them to log in to the career site.

Thanks,

Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Referral Process – Enabling Email Notification to Ref...
entry.content:
Hi Margherita,

Please check the applicant status configuration. For each status, there is an option to configure an email for the referee. Once the candidate is moved to that stage, an email will be automatically triggered to the referee.

Thanks,

Sanjeet

entry.title: S4/HANA Cloud - How to block Customers and Supplier from posting.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm working on a project in S4/HANA Cloud Public Edition and I need to block suppliers and customers from posting. I found a Business Object that maintains Business Partners, but it doesn't have a 'Posting Block' option. The closest solution I've found is an ODATA V2 API that has this option within its structure. I would prefer doing this with a Business Object; do you know if there is one that can do the job?


Business Object doc link: https://api.sap.com/bointerface/I_BUSINESSPARTNERTP_3

ODATA V2 API doc link: https://api.sap.com/api/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/overview


Kind Regards,
Marcelo Romeiro

entry.title: Re: What is the best practice when adding a candidate in...
entry.content: Hello Sanjeet - thank you very much for the feedback. Appreciate it! I do believe we need to turn this on and it should help in this scenario. Thank you again. Corey

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ Quote 2.0 Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud...

You have two options:

* replication from S4hana

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Creating a Supplier Hierarchy ?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We implementing MDG on S/4 HANA, where we need to create Hierarchies for Supplier master data. As per my little understanding, I think first we need to create the hierarchies in MDG and then the task of assigning suppliers or business partners to the hierarchies will come. Should we be creating those hierarchies at BP level or supplier level. 

Can any one of you tell me or guide me as to how we should create this hierarchy for supplier. I can see a lot of CR Types available from standard.

1) which CR types to use from the following. My assumption says either BPHP1  or BPHP2 to be used. I could be wrong too.

                    BP1P2                  Create Bus. Partner w. Hry. Assignment

                    BPHP1                  Process Business Partner Hierarchies 

                    BPHP2                  Process Business Partner Hierarchies

                    SUPPL1P2           Create Supplier w. Hierarchy Assignment

2) Since we are doing this in MDG on S/4 HANA, should we create hierarchies at BP level or supplier level?  

Thanks, T

entry.title: Re: Update sales order item route on save
entry.content:
Hi PraveenDasari,

please try make change in USEREXIT_MOVE_FIELD_TO_VBKD of userexit MV45AFZZ -> modify XVBAP data. 

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Re: Debugging hints for DRC eInvoice - Mexico CFDI example
entry.content: Great content !!! Really appreciated.

entry.title: Re: "Error while parsing an XML stream" while trying to create customer for Business partn
entry.content:
I even used to pass it all together like we do in Odata Gateway payload. But got the same error.

BP Role, Customer creation script using SET_KEY :

SET_KEY(SET_KEY(SET_KEY(data.A_BusinessPartner1,"to_BusinessPartnerAddress", [ {BusinessPartner: params.BPID, AddressID: params.AddressID, HouseNumber: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.HouseNumber, StreetName: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.StreetName, CityName: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.CityName, Country: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.Country, PostalCode: data.A_BusinessPartnerAddress1.PostalCode} ] ), "to_BusinessPartnerRole", [ {BusinessPartnerRole: "FLCU00"}, {BusinessPartnerRole: "FLCU01"} ]), “to_Customer”,  SET_KEY( {Customer: "", CustomerAccountGroup: "ZBLD"}, "to_CustomerCompany", [ SET_KEY( {Customer: "", CompanyCode: "US00", CustomerAccountGroup: "ZBLD", ReconciliationAccount: "110000", PaymentTerms: "0001", APARToleranceGroup: "", PaymentBlockingReason: ""} ) ])

entry.title: Assembly of Unplanned (non-BOM) Materials
entry.content:
Introduction

Assembling unplanned (non-BOM) materials is a common requirement for any manufacturing process, which might happen in situations like:

1. Operator Discretion During Rework
An operator identifies a defect and replaces a component with a similar but not pre-listed one (e.g., a resistor of equivalent rating but from a different vendor). The new component wasn’t originally in the BOM but was necessary to proceed.
✅ Used when reacting to real-time production deviations.

2. Engineering Changes Not Yet Reflected in the BOM
A design or material change has been approved but not yet updated in the system. The shop floor team uses the new component proactively based on verbal or preliminary instructions.
✅ Common during rapid prototyping or early production ramp-up.

3. Component Substitution Due to Inventory Shortages
The planned BOM component is out of stock, and a compatible alternative is pulled from inventory to keep the line running and avoid downtime.
✅ Practical workaround to maintain productivity in the face of supply chain issues.

4. Assembly Aids or Temporary Materials
Temporary components like protective caps, alignment jigs, or glue used during assembly may not be listed in the BOM but are still consumed.
✅ Frequently happens in manual or semi-automated assembly processes.

5. Quality Assurance Additions
QA may require additional test components or sensors temporarily added for inspection or calibration that are not part of the standard BOM.
✅ Often seen in high-spec manufacturing environments like aerospace or medical devices.

In this blog, I will show you how this can be done via /assembledComponents API call, using isBomComponentAssembly flag, which is 'true' by default, but can be set to 'false', enabling non-BOM component assembly.

Implementation

Create and configure a Production Process to call 'Assemble Components' service, /assembledComponents API, and set isBomComponentAssembly flag as 'false', along with all other mandatory input fields. Save and Deploy it!

Of course, you can also perform the call to /assembledComponents API from Postman or any other 3rd party software for learning or testing purposes, from Custom POD Plugins, from Core POD Plugins Extensions or from any 3rd party systems in a real life scenario.

Execution

Run the Production Process with proper input parameters, to execute the API call, which should run successfully.

Double check in Monitor Production Process app, that the execution was successful.

A successful API call should trigger 'Production Order Component Add' integration message back to ERP:

Which triggers the Goods Issue posting in ERP, consuming the material MZ_BR_T1_R1:

Which was initially not planned the the order Bill of Materials (BOM):

Conclusion

Using isBomComponentAssembly flag in /assembledComponents API, it´s possible to consume unplanned (non-BOM Components) in a discrete manufacturing flow.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.
Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Custom Code decommission - Transaction usage
entry.content: Hello All - we are performing the analysis of transaction that are not used in Production and are candidate for decommission, the challenge we are facing the user base confirm on transaction is used bt the cockpit is showing no usage , how and what to use to get right amount of information

entry.title: Re: How to add a new field to Business Volume Selection Criteria?

Hello,

I followed the exact same steps. However, I don't see the new field in the dropdown for selection in the business volume in condition contract. What would be the reason? Am I missing any step.

entry.title: Re: How to assign roles to Consolidation steps ?
entry.content: I am still looking for an answer to this post.
